findings.message: post_item() accepts user-supplied input and writes it to the database via the bare create_item(), bypassing create_item_normalized(). The repository explicitly designates create_item_normalized() as the canonical entry-point for user-supplied names ("Callers that accept user input MUST go through this function, not the bare create_item()"), and create_item()'s own docstring says "Use create_item_normalized for user-supplied input." This route is the canonical user-input path yet deliberately skips the strip+lowercase normalization, so user names enter the persistent store untransformed. The result is an inconsistent data store: 'Foo', ' foo ', and 'foo' all persist as distinct values, breaking the stated casing/whitespace consistency invariant and any downstream uniqueness/lookup logic that assumes normalized names.
findings.suggestion: Call create_item_normalized(session, payload.name) instead of create_item(session, payload.name) so user-supplied names are normalized before they reach the store, per the repository's documented contract.
